cover: System architecture diagram: 'Salesforce Data Cloud Vector Search: Semantic Case Router'. Light grey background with faint grid. Dark bold header block 'Semantic Case Router on Data Cloud Vector Search'. Subtitle 'Case text → vector search → queue confidence → Agentforce 2.0 handoff'. Top-right: profile badge 'Bennie Joseph | Architect'. LAYER 1 'Salesforce Service Cloud' (pastel blue): modules 'Case Subject + Description', 'Product + Entitlement', 'Omni-Channel Queue'. LAYER 2 'Data Cloud Native Vector Search' (pastel purple): modules 'Routing_Corpus__dlm', 'Case Embeddings', 'VECTOR_DISTANCE Top 10'. LAYER 3 'Decision Layer' (pastel green): modules 'Confidence Score', 'Queue Mapping', 'Human Override'. Central node 'Semantic Router' with rotating arrow, dotted directional connectors to all layers. INTEGRATION LAYER (upper right): 'Agentforce 2.0 Custom Action' and 'Salesforce API v64.0'. BOTTOM PANEL 'Production Guardrails': sub-modules 'Thresholds', 'Audit Log', 'Fallback Queue' with flow arrows. Rounded rectangle containers, dotted arrowheads, micro vector icons, modern sans-serif fonts, publication-ready engineering diagram.
images:
A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "DATA CLOUD VECTOR SEARCH CODE".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "DATA CLOUD VECTOR SEARCH CODE".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "The Exact SOQL Pattern for Semantic Case Routing in Production" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "Semantic Match" with a target icon, "Score Threshold" with a filter icon, "Fallback Queue" with a shield icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "OLD WAY — Keyword Assignment Rules". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "NEW WAY — VECTOR_DISTANCE Semantic Query". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    // Keyword rules: brittle and incomplete
// IF Subject CONTAINS 'refund' → Billing Queue
// IF Subject CONTAINS 'error' → Tech Support
// IF Subject CONTAINS 'warranty' → Warranty
// Misses: "product stopped working" → wrong queue
// Misses: "charge on account" → not 'refund'
// Result: 40% mis-routing rate in production
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    SELECT Routing_Queue__c, Confidence_Score__c
FROM Routing_Corpus__dlm
WHERE VECTOR_DISTANCE(
  Case_Embedding__c,
  :queryCaseEmbedding,
  'COSINE'
) < 0.25
ORDER BY VECTOR_DISTANCE(...) ASC
LIMIT 10
// Top match queue used if score >= 0.75

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "Embed case subject + description together, not separately", "Routing corpus: resolved cases + manual routing examples", "Reject routing if top VECTOR_DISTANCE score exceeds 0.25", "Fallback queue required — never leave a case unrouted", "Re-embed corpus quarterly as routing patterns evolve".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "Data Cloud", "VECTOR_DISTANCE", "Case Routing", "Agentforce 2.0" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.

A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "SEMANTIC ROUTER PRODUCTION GUARDRAILS".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "SEMANTIC ROUTER PRODUCTION GUARDRAILS".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "Confidence Gates, Fallback Queues, and Audit for Data Cloud Routing" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "Score Threshold" with a filter icon, "Fallback Queue" with a shield icon, "Full Audit Trail" with a key icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "NO GUARDRAILS — All Cases Auto-Routed Blindly". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "PRODUCTION GUARDRAILS — Threshold + Fallback + Audit". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    // Auto-routing without confidence check (wrong)
const match = await vectorSearch(caseEmbedding)
await assignQueue(caseId, match.queue)
// No score threshold. Low-confidence cases routed.
// No audit. No fallback. No human override path.
// Customer escalates → you cannot explain routing.
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    const results = await vectorSearch(caseEmbedding)
const top = results[0]
if (!top || top.confidence < 0.75) {
  await assignFallbackQueue(caseId)
  await auditLog.record('LOW_CONFIDENCE', top)
  return  // human reviews in default queue
}
await assignQueue(caseId, top.routingQueue)
await auditLog.record('AUTO_ROUTED', top)

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "Set confidence threshold at 0.75 — tune on real production data", "Every case must have a fallback queue — never leave unrouted", "Log: caseId, matched corpusId, distance score, queue assigned", "Human override UI: agent can correct routing + flag for re-training", "Monthly: review low-confidence cases to improve corpus coverage".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "Data Cloud Vector Search", "Confidence Gate", "Audit Log", "Agentforce 2.0" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.
